Defendant Albay Construction Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-31-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq. of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Albay Construction Co.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Gary Hubbell

RULING:
Defendant Albay Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production, and even if it had, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether plaintiff inhaled respirable asbestos fibers from products and/or materials attributable to defendant. The mere fact that plaintiff wore a paper mask does not require granting defendant's motion. Mr. Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue on this point. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

Defendant Todd Shipyard Corp.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael J. Peng, Esq. of YARON & ASSOCIATES (415-658-2929), appearing for defendant / moving party Todd Shipyard Corp.
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Gary Hubbell

RULING:
Defendant Todd Shipyards Corp.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication are denied. As to plaintiff's claim for premises owner liability, plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. As to the sophisticated user defense, assuming without deciding that some variant of the sophisticated user/intermediary defense might potentially be applicable to plaintiff's claim, defendant has not established either the knowledge or constructive knowledge of either plaintiff or his employer about the hazards of the asbestos that he was exposed to by defendant or the knowledge of defendant regarding what was known or should have been known by either plaintiff or his employer about the hazards of asbestos that plaintiff was exposed to by defendant, so any such defense has not been established as a matter of law. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
